잠언 16:1.content: The plans of the heart belong to man, but the answer of the tongue is from Yahweh.

잠언 16:2.content: All the ways of a man are clean in his own eyes; but Yahweh weighs the motives.

잠언 16:3.content: Commit your deeds to Yahweh, and your plans shall succeed.

잠언 16:4.content: Yahweh has made everything for its own end— yes, even the wicked for the day of evil.

잠언 16:5.content: Everyone who is proud in heart is an abomination to Yahweh: they shall certainly not be unpunished.

잠언 16:6.content: By mercy and truth iniquity is atoned for. By the fear of Yahweh men depart from evil.

잠언 16:7.content: When a man’s ways please Yahweh, he makes even his enemies to be at peace with him.

잠언 16:8.content: Better is a little with righteousness, than great revenues with injustice.

잠언 16:9.content: A man’s heart plans his course, but Yahweh directs his steps.

잠언 16:10.content: Inspired judgments are on the lips of the king. He shall not betray his mouth.

잠언 16:11.content: Honest balances and scales are Yahweh’s; all the weights in the bag are his work.

잠언 16:12.content: It is an abomination for kings to do wrong, for the throne is established by righteousness.

잠언 16:13.content: Righteous lips are the delight of kings. They value one who speaks the truth.

잠언 16:14.content: The king’s wrath is a messenger of death, but a wise man will pacify it.

잠언 16:15.content: In the light of the king’s face is life. His favor is like a cloud of the spring rain.

잠언 16:16.content: How much better it is to get wisdom than gold! Yes, to get understanding is to be chosen rather than silver.

잠언 16:17.content: The highway of the upright is to depart from evil. He who keeps his way preserves his soul.

잠언 16:18.content: Pride goes before destruction, and an arrogant spirit before a fall.

잠언 16:19.content: It is better to be of a lowly spirit with the poor, than to divide the plunder with the proud.

잠언 16:20.content: He who heeds the Word finds prosperity. Whoever trusts in Yahweh is blessed.

잠언 16:21.content: The wise in heart shall be called prudent. Pleasantness of the lips promotes instruction.

잠언 16:22.content: Understanding is a fountain of life to one who has it, but the punishment of fools is their folly.

잠언 16:23.content: The heart of the wise instructs his mouth, and adds learning to his lips.

잠언 16:24.content: Pleasant words are a honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health to the bones.

잠언 16:25.content: There is a way which seems right to a man, but in the end it leads to death.

잠언 16:26.content: The appetite of the laboring man labors for him; for his mouth urges him on.

잠언 16:27.content: A worthless man devises mischief. His speech is like a scorching fire.

잠언 16:28.content: A perverse man stirs up strife. A whisperer separates close friends.

잠언 16:29.content: A man of violence entices his neighbor, and leads him in a way that is not good.

잠언 16:30.content: One who winks his eyes to plot perversities, one who compresses his lips, is bent on evil.

잠언 16:31.content: Gray hair is a crown of glory. It is attained by a life of righteousness.

잠언 16:32.content: One who is slow to anger is better than the mighty; one who rules his spirit, than he who takes a city.

잠언 16:33.content: The lot is cast into the lap, but its every decision is from Yahweh.
